pattern: Sorting first lets a backtracking search skip duplicate candidates at each level, so every unique combination is produced exactly once.
hints: Each number may be used at most once, and the input can contain repeats, so the real challenge is avoiding duplicate combinations rather than finding sums.
Sort the candidates; then duplicates sit next to each other and you can decide, per recursion level, to skip a value you have already tried at that level.
Backtrack from a start index, adding candidates[i] and recursing from i+1; at a given level, if candidates[i] == candidates[i-1] skip it, and break once candidates[i] exceeds the remaining target.

edge_inputs.description: duplicates in input, multiple distinct combinations
repeated value used to its available multiplicity
a single element equals the target
all identical values, several reach target

stress.description: small multiset with heavy duplication so the dedup logic is exercised
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(4, min(12, max(4, size)))
    candidates = [rng.randint(1, 8) for _ in range(n)]
    target = rng.randint(1, 20)
    return [candidates, target]
stress.note: kept small because the brute-force oracle enumerates subsets
